response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS applications) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second, which demonstrates the distributed-systems and performance mindset the role requires. However, modern C++ experience is limited and dated (a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), there is no Rust, and no direct navigation/routing pipeline experience. Recent years have been predominantly TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, making the fit for a C++-primary routing tiling role uncertain despite strong adjacent signals.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data experience as founder of an open-source GIS platform used in production
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) in a compiled language (DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and knowledge-sharing track record
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct experience with routing engines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent work is web/full-stack TypeScript product development, far from systems-level data pipeline engineering
